That being said, if someone does follow those things and by the end of the year you should be more than happy to help them out. I don't think that it's really realistic that anyone who uses supplements, eats the food and checks in on the cadence that they're supposed to for an entire year would not get results but even if they did for some reason not get results you would still be jattering sufficient revenue from them that it would more than pay for the amount of time that it would take you to continue to serve them to get them to their end goal.
